stonetop.summary: They were, perhaps, the first of the Makers .

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body:
They were, perhaps, the first of the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.eoTIBDMIzJXByPCZ]{Makers}. They charted the seas and the stars. They leashed the elements to their will. They built mighty ships and flying castles, sailed forth from their island home and conquered. All who stood against them were reduced to ash and glass and slag.

The pride of the Tempest Lords was warranted, but their hubris led to their fall. They sought to tear their entire homeland free from the earth, to set it floating above the world they ruled.

They failed. Calamity followed.

The seas boiled and belched forth horrors. Fire rained from the sky. Their island capital sank beneath the waves, and the spine of their empire was shattered. Civil war erupted. Colonies rebelled.

The Tempest Lords who survived were scattered to the winds. Some tried to restore the empire, but met violent ends at the hands of rivals or would-be subjects. Soon, only a few Tempest Lords remained, enigmas even among other Makers.

One of these survivors, Stormcatcher, made her way to the the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.nBvsH19B1bG2d4Di]{Flats}, beyond even the most far-flung of the empire’s old outposts. There, between four other nations of Makers, she built her tower. And when the horrors of the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.5Kym1qDHTmM8XUQ6]{Things Below} were set loose upon the world, Stormcatcher’s tower—what folks now call the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.iYSktNtms4NQat8F]{Ruined Tower}— was the last of the Makers’ enclaves to fall.

sections.heading: Themes
sections.body:
Pick, or roll 1d12.

RollResult
1Elemental forces and primordial power: wielded and harnessed, perhaps imperfectly
2Storms: thunder and lightning, wrath and fury, power unleashed
3The heavens: weather and clouds, sky-islands, stars and celestial bodies
4Sailing: seas and ships, navigation and exploration
5Machines and devices, aetherium and stored energy
6Curiosity/experimentation/ invention/hubris
7Loss of control: unexpected consequences/berserk rage/cataclysmic failure/experiments run amok
8An empire collapsed: conquering armies, colonies, plunder, lost glory, resentment
9–12Sized for giants, and roll 1d8 again

sections.heading: Language and names
sections.body:
Tempest Lord writing was ideogrammatic (characters represent ideas, not sounds), and they spoke a primordial language—all who heard it understood it as their native tongue. As such, the names of Tempest Lords are rendered as descriptions, titles, or honorifics.

Example names: Brinedrinker, Eyes- Like-Lightning, Fardweller, Foehammer, Lightning-Is-Her-Spear, His-Smile-Isa-Harbor, Mind-Like-Fire, Moonspeaker, Oarbreaker, Rainchaser, Soother-Of- Waves, Sparkspitter, Squalltalker, Starfinder, Taller-Than-Masts, Voice-of-Thunder, Wavecutter, Waymaker, Windwalker

When other @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.eoTIBDMIzJXByPCZ]{Makers} spoke or wrote of them, they used their own tongue. Windwalker was called Vetrodokha by the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.VosjScKksp5B3hsc]{Green Lords} and Avaelkerzhaer by the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.rJiU3RhbILojTC2u]{Stone Lords}.

sections.heading: Sites
sections.body:
Pick, or have someone roll the Die of Fate.

1-3 Lingering signs of their presence (see below) 4-6 A ruin

Lingering signs Pick, or have someone roll the Die of Fate.

1d6 sign

RollResult
1A place bearing scars of unchecked power
2Fragments of a ship or sky-island
3–4Enigmatic structures
5An elemental spirit of sky, storm, weather, etc.; bound and tethered
6Ruins of other @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.eoTIBDMIzJXByPCZ]{Makers} or the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.JDCcrvQhMYFFlUIe]{Barrow Builders}: blasted, shattered, vitrified

Scars of unchecked power For example:

  • A crater, lousy with fulgurites and shards of glass
  • A fractal, branching pattern etched in a bare expanse of stone
  • A rune-etched menhir or large hunk of makerglass, near which some fundamental law or force (time, gravity, space, light, etc.) is warped
  • A barren stretch of glass pebbles, strangely colored and always warm
  • The vitrified remains of a Maker, a monster, a small army, etc. Crude glass shapes hinting at what they once were, flash-frozen in time

Places scarred by such power might very well be radioactive, haunted sites, and/or corrupted by the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.5Kym1qDHTmM8XUQ6]{Things Below}.

Fragments of a ship or sky-island For example:

  • A ship’s hull, mostly buried but somehow still intact
  • A torn sail of aetherium mesh, strapped to a rusty metal cross
  • Strange objects, strewn about and long-buried: a crumpled brass cylinder, an etched metal disk, an oversized ship’s wheel, a rudder, etc.
  • A rocky outcrop, the stone alien to the surrounding terrain, tilted at an odd angle
  • A cracked slab of granite, big as the granary and shot through with aetherium, floating a few feet above the ground and spinning listlessly
  • Slabs of worked stone: broken, buried, and scattered, like a child’s toy dashed from impossible heights

Enigmatic structures For example:

  • Iron spires, like hollow rusted trees, placed in a line across the landscape
  • A black cube the size of a house; when lightning strikes, the earth lights up for miles with glowing lines that fade over the next few minutes
  • Stone plinths along a ridge line, some of them topped with 6-foot-tall sculptures, like tulips, spinning in the breeze
  • Copper cylinders, each maybe 8 feet tall and caked in verdigris; mostly buried in white and chalky soil
  • Megaliths, etched with ideographs that describe the motions of the stars
  • A bronze plaque upon a stone slab, the only notable feature for miles; untouched by lichen; deeply etched ideographs begin with the phrase, “This is not a place of honor”

A ruin Pick or roll for themes, structure, purpose, architectural elements, and condition. Place the ruin in an appropriate terrain (see page 126 for the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.nBvsH19B1bG2d4Di]{Flats} and page 350 for the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.apczJSWiu0M5dFrF]{South Manmarch}, but Tempest Lords ranged far and wide). Populate it with lingering signs, artifacts and/or dangers of the Tempest Lords or the appropriate region.

RollResult
1–2Cluster of smaller buildings around some central feature
3Single, larger building (block, dome, ziggurat, etc.)
4Bunker/vault carved into the stone
5Platform/amphitheater/terrace(s)
6Tower/spire
7–9Sky-island, crashed/grounded (and roll d6 again)
10–11Sky-island, afloat but anchored/ fixed over a particular place (and roll d6 again)
12Sky-island, free-floating across the landscape (and roll d6 again)

1d12 purpose

RollResult
1–2Dwelling (home, barracks, dormitory, etc.)
3–4Infrastructure (cistern, sewer, lighthouse, harbor, etc.)
5–6Storage (vault, batteries, warehouse, library, museum, etc.)
7–8Production (generator, workshop, shipworks, factory, farm, etc.)
9–10Study/Experimentation (laboratory, observatory, orrery, etc.)
11–12Enigma (magical device, artistic endeavor, etc.)

1d12 architectural elements

RollResult
1–2Metal beams/cables/lattices/trusses
3–4Open spaces: shafts, atriums, vaulted ceilings, overlooks, windows, skylights
5–6Lines of power: aetherium inlays, runes, conduits, nodes, currents
7–8Stylized mosaics/statuary: geometric patterns, fractals, celestial bodies, angelic beings, ships
9–10Brass fixtures, pipes and pumps, water and steam, gears and flywheels
11–12Verticality: standing stones, menhirs, obelisks, towers, spires, masts

Most Maker-ruins are sized for giants— people two or even three times as tall as humans—but often with smaller-scale rooms, passages, and so forth for their servants.

1d12 condition

RollResult
1–4Shattered and laid waste by time, disaster, or vandalism; mostly buried, overgrown, collapsed; its purpose unrecognizable
5–8Crumbling, largely buried, overgrown, or hidden away
9–10Buried, overgrown, or hidden, but mostly intact
11–12Fully visible and strangely well-preserved

For more examples, see the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.iYSktNtms4NQat8F]{Ruined Tower}.

One of the PCs is heading to @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.fc9yqAlxCCgZVckC]{Titan Bones}, and I’d like some sense of what’s out there. I think it holds a Tempest Lord ruin, probably once associated with Stormcatcher and the now-Ruined Tower.

Stormcatcher erected a spire on Titan Bones, like a lighthouse, topped with an aetherium weathervane that modulated the weather. When the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.rJiU3RhbILojTC2u]{Stone Lords}’ rebellious servants attacked, the tower’s custodian took the weathervane and hid beneath the spire. The spire fell and the custodian was trapped. All that remains are scattered stones and a circular foundation. And beneath centuries of dirt and rubble, there’s a locked trapdoor. It reveals a spiral staircase, a deep shaft, an intact cellar, a skeleton, and an aetherium weathervane.

I roll a 3 for theme (“the heavens: weather and clouds...”), a 4 for structure (“bunker/ vault”), a 3 for purpose (“infrastructure”), and an 11 for architectural elementals (“verticality”). I then roll a 5 for condition (“largely buried... hidden”). I ponder a bit and come up with this:

Aetherium

An alloy of copper and lightning. Pure aetherium is light, soft, and ductile (similar to tin), but the Tempest Lords and @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.raJkZVRsiVDqsgbj]{Forge Lords} could further alloy it or manipulate it with magic to have a wide range of properties and uses.

Pick or roll for form and characteristic.

1d12 form

  • 1-3 Raw material (ingot/spool of wire/ rod/plate/lamé/shard/etc.) 4-5 Enigmatic shape (torus/orb/tube/ hoop/runes/etc.), its purpose unclear 6-7 Mundane object (tool/vessel/ lantern/etc.), though perhaps not recognized as such 8-9 Art or decoration (jewel/statue/ inlay/torc/amulet/ring/etc.) 10 Clothing/armor (robe/tunic/apron/ cuirass/brigandine/etc.) 11 Weapon (spear/arrow/sword/maul/ shield/trident/wand/etc.) 12 Construction (archway/truss/beam/ door/plating/pipes/etc.), likely immobile
  • Consider rolling 1d6 to inform size/scale.

1d12 characteristic

RollResult
1Breathtakingly beautiful
2Hard and strong as bronze
3Absorbs/stores/discharges/ transforms an elemental force
4Suppresses/repels/directs/impedes a particular element/force
5Vibrates in ways that interact with spirits/magic
6Extremely light/levitates/defies or reverses gravity
7Attunes to another item/locale, for communication/detection
8Roll 1d8 twice
9Unstable, prone to discharge/failure (dangerous) and roll d8 again
10Relatively soft/ductile/malleable (crude)
11–12Roll again with 1d8, but it becomes unstable and/or soft (as above) after any serious use

Making aetherium A clever arcanist might rediscover the process of making aetherium. To do so, you must…

  • Study a largely-intact aetherium crucible for a few weeks
  • Study both active and depleted aetherium for a few weeks
  • Consult a knowledgeable metalsmith (or be one yourself)
  • Acquire the appropriate chemics (Value 0) and a sizeable mass of copper (Value 1, immobile)
  • Devise some way to introduce lightning to molten copper
  • Risk injury, fire, destruction, and failure

When you meet all the requirements, you can transform a purse of coppers (Value 0) and the appropriate chemics (Value 0) into an ingot or casting of pure aetherium (magical, crude, possibly dangerous, Value 1 to those-who-know). The process is risky; you are Defying Danger each time you undertake it.

To imbue aetherium with one of the characteristics above, you’ll need access to a trove of Tempest Lord or Forge Lord lore, at least. Beyond that, Make a Plan with the GM.

Steading ImprovementAETHERIUM CRUCIBLE

Requires all of the following:

  • Learning the process of making aetherium
  • Learning to imbue aetherium with at least one special characteristic
  • A reliable means of capturing, storing, and directing lightning that strikes the Stone
  • Securing a steady supply of the chemics needed to make aetherium
  • Securing a steady supply of copper
  • An exceptional smith who knows the secrets of aetherium

When you mark all the requirements, add “Aetherium Crucible” to the Resources list and the map. As long as you trade aetherium to the outside world, increase Prosperity by 1. Henceforth, small amounts of raw aetherium (magical, Value 1) are commonly available in @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.6yScslDfqrcCQ6CJ]{Stonetop}, and you have advantage to Trade & Barter for the aetherium items with special characteristics (+2 Value).

sections.heading: Associated Creatures
sections.body:
Creatures the bestiary catalogues for Tempest Lords. Click through for full stat blocks and lore.

  • @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.qUp7ZkpC5FdinUC4]{Elemental Vortex}

sections.heading: Hooks
sections.body:
• A terrible storm sweeps through, a sky-island floating in its eye
  • A scholar believes that @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.6yScslDfqrcCQ6CJ]{Stonetop} hides a trove of Tempest Lord artifacts
  • Cultists seek an orrery with the power to change the stars, to hasten a conjunction that will free their master
  • Bandits unearth a Tempest Lord weapon and threaten Stonetop with it
  • A hdour seeks to revive a vitrified Maker

sections.heading: Artifacts
sections.body:
Various treasures

  • A small lump of aetherium (Value 0 to most, Value 1 to those-who-know)
  • An oversized aetherium spear-head, dull and soft (awkward, crude, Value 1)
  • A big brass locket, with a needle inside that points towards a specific Tempest Lord site (Value 2)
  • A few bolts of canvas, the material unfamiliar but very sturdy (Value 2)
  • A thunderstorm trapped in a big makerglass bottle, capped with aetherium (dangerous, Value 3)
  • A few tarnished silver coins from the Tempest Lords’ empire (Value 1 to most, Value 3 to the right collector)
  • A codex of thin, metallic sheets, stamped with star charts and Tempest Lord ideographs (Value 3)
  • A wall-sized bronze plaque, showing a map of the entire Tempest Lord empire (immobile, Value 4)

Aetherium urn

, magical, dangerous The lid is held fast by no apparent means. It takes some effort to pry open and eagerly snaps back into place.

When the urn is open and empty, it draws raw elemental force into it like a lodestone draws iron. With the lid in place, the force is safely stored. If the urn is damaged or dented (not hard), any stored force escapes, violently.

Bronze ring

  • , magical, fireproof, hours About 8 inches in diameter, an inch thick throughout, scorched and smeared with ash. A button protrudes, with a Tempest Lord glyph that might mean “stubborn.”

When you find the ring, it has enough power to run for 1d4-1 hours.

When you press the button, the ring fixes in place, defying gravity. It simply will not budge. The effect ends if the ring is bent or broken, or when it runs out of power. There’s no obvious way to end the effect early. When the ring runs out of power, the button pops out and the effect ends.

When you heat the ring in a forge or kiln for about eight hours, it gains an hour of use (up to 3 hours).

Floating sash magical A length of bluish-white lamé, maybe 6 inches wide by 6 feet long, snaking about in the air. Like a belt, but for someone far larger than you.

When you wrap the sash securely around your waist, it buoys you against falls—you never fall faster than if you had fallen your own height.

Silvery tuning fork magical, loud When you strike this tuning fork against a hard surface, it emits a loud tone for about a minute. Nearby (reach, area) magical effects and creatures become visible: shaking, vibrating, distorted. The noise draws the attention of any spirits or magically attuned beings for miles around.

Storm-cloud potion magical, fragile A glass vial of dark-grey liquid, roiling and shot through with sparks.

When you drink a storm-cloud potion, lightning courses through you. Take 1d10 damage (ignores armor) and mark a debility. Should you survive, hold 3 Storm.

While you hold any Storm, you cannot feel pain or fear, tell friend from foe, speak coherently, or exercise patience or care. Add your Storm to your Armor, and your melee and thrown attacks do +Storm damage (+loud, +forceful).

When you reduce someone or something to 0 HP, lose 1 Storm.

  • Minor arcana
  • Pick 1 or have someone roll.

1d6 minor arcanum

RollResult
1A half-buried plaque
2A cloak, richly embroidered
3A corroded spearhead
4A copper-banded staff
5An odd conveyance
6A bow with no string
  • Major arcana
  • These might very well be associated with the Tempest Lords.
  • @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-items.Item.65NmuJ13p1U6Qtf0]{The Azure Hand}
  • @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-items.Item.HViXYPwjsyjftTq4]{Ineffable Words}
  • @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-items.Item.JFa1QzEN46tZeeJ0]{Storm Markings}

groups.body:
Monsters and entities

  • A thunder drake or luglfsk, drawn to aetherium
  • Ghosts or revenants, victims of the Tempest Lord’s experiments, callousness, or wrath
  • An archon, ensuring some Tempest Lord artifact goes unused
  • An elemental spirit or a fundamental, tethered to a device or artifice
  • A primordial entity or something corrupted by the @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.5Kym1qDHTmM8XUQ6]{Things Below}, vitrified and held in stasis.
